flagged_setups.thesis: Pre-earnings entry at $214.34 AH (T-8 to May 20 Q1 FY2027 report). Consensus: $78.5B revenue +78.2% YoY, EPS $1.76 +116.9% YoY. AMD $10.25B Q1 +38% validates merchant silicon DC demand; hyperscaler $725B capex 2026 (+77%) is the strongest demand floor since 2024. Goldman $250 PT, consensus $272, 37/37 Strong Buy. Risk: PLTR-style valuation selloff even on beat; China export control wildcard from Beijing summit; NVDA historically falls more often than rises post-earnings even on beats.

narrative_threads: Warsh inheritance: Fed Chair confirmed to Board today; Chair vote this week; inherits PCE 4.5%, ISM Prices 84.6%, CPI 3.7% est today, Brent $105 — the exact conditions that make easing politically demanded but monetarily impossible

Hormuz resolution timing: Trump rejected Iran's latest proposal ('massive life support'); Citi end-May thesis intact but deal is not imminent; each week of delay = another month of elevated CPI; this is the core inflation driver in today's number

NVDA May 20 countdown: AMD set the bar ($10.25B Q1 +38%); hyperscaler $725B capex signals peak demand; Goldman beat-and-raise thesis vs PLTR selloff-on-beat pattern; $214 AH is a pre-earnings dip entry opportunity
Triple ATH session (May 11): SPX 7,412 + NDX 26,274 + RUT 2,868 all record closes — broad participation confirms bull cycle intact despite oil headwind and macro uncertainty
Beijing CEO delegation: 17 CEOs including Musk, Cook, Fink signals US corporate engagement with China at peak level; Iran agenda dominates over tariffs; trade truce extension modest but stabilizing
